phase_status: JUDGMENT-DAY ROUND 1 FOUND THREE REAL addImport DEFECTS that three verify-final iterations AND the four-persona Council all missed. The blind gate earned its keep a second time. Root cause (one, not three): REQ-RXD-05 step 1's already-bound check had a wrong notion of 'bound' — it compared LOCAL names, ignored type-only-ness, and only considered the SAME module. (1) CRITICAL: `import type { Icon }` + addImport('Icon','./icons') -> silent no-op; Icon never value-bound; erased at compile -> ReferenceError at runtime. EXACTLY the class V5's changelog claimed to close — V5 fixed the MERGE (step 2, which correctly excludes type-only) but step 1 runs FIRST and never reaches it. Both judges' mutation tables independently showed the isTypeOnly guard killing only 1 of ~196 tests (denylist kills 17, reserved-words kills 11) — Judge A chased the weak signal and found the bug; Judge B logged it and moved on. (2) WARNING(real): cross-module duplicate — `import { useState } from 'react'` + addImport('useState','./local') emits code node rejects with SyntaxError: Identifier already declared. REQ-RXD-05's headline says 'MUST NEVER produce an invalid or duplicate binding' UNQUALIFIED, but the algorithm only reasoned about the same module — the spec contradicted itself and the code followed the steps while violating the requirement. (3) WARNING(real): aliased — `import { Foo as x }` + addImport('x','./a') -> silent no-op, every reference to x resolves to Foo. V6 step 1 explicitly SANCTIONED this. ORCHESTRATOR ERROR: I relayed Judge A's claim that `import { Foo as x, x }` is legal. It is NOT — verified myself: it fails at PARSE time (SyntaxError: Identifier 'x' has already been declared), while the control `import { Foo as x, y }` gets past parsing and fails only at module resolution. sdd-spec tested the premise rather than trusting my brief, and that correction COLLAPSED defects 2 and 3 into one mechanism (a claimed local name). It also proved with tsc that the naive type-only fix I suggested (exclude type-only, fall through to CREATE) is itself unsafe: `import type { Icon }` + `import { Icon }` = TS2300 Duplicate identifier.
next_phase: steward_reckoning (purpose-lifecycle gate, BLIND — read skills/_shared/purpose-lifecycle.md first, never from memory; judges the delivered change against triage.md's ORIGINAL problem_statement, halts outcome-gap) -> arch_refresh_post_verify (impact additive -> prompt refresh of sdd/{project}/architecture) -> arch_audit_gate (violations -> halt) -> sdd-archive (accepts pass-with-followups; Step 9 registers verify-report.md §9's 18-item ledger; sync delta specs into main specs; move folder to archive/; lessons-learned; ADR promotion recommendations). POST-ARCHIVE COMMITMENTS: /plan for the TS-dialect addImport fix (owner: 'apenas archive corremos /plan ahí mismo'); react-dictionary DX idea parked at obs #2296.
spec_detail: V8, owner-signed 2026-07-17 (both judgment calls explicitly endorsed at sign time: the 09.6 'most common trigger' characterization, and the TS-dialect followup as a NEW archive-ledger bullet distinct from item 22). V8 CLOSES THE MODEL, adopting rather than inventing: CLAIMED(name) = (a) local name of ANY import specifier, any module, value or type-only (V7 half, unchanged) OR (b) a top-level VALUE-NAMESPACE declaration — function/const/let/var/class/enum/namespace (ADR-0039's assertNoCollision predicate verbatim, typescript/ops.ts:42-75). type/interface exempt in both halves. Syntactic, file-wide, not scope-aware — ADR-0039's established posture. WHY THE VUELTAS HAPPENED (owner-ratified diagnosis): three gate rounds found the same class on new axes because each fix derived the model EMPIRICALLY, one bug at a time, while the complete model was already written in ADR-0039 — and even the TS dialect's own addImport does not use the assertNoCollision that sits 20 lines below it. React's addImport was copied from TS's naive ten-liner (react/ops.ts:9 says so), which carries the same defect family, unaudited, because its spec never promised an invariant. That debt is now registered SHARPENED: the TS dialect does not merely lack validation — it can EMIT INVALID BINDINGS. claimedNameTail ruling: the load-bearing error was REQ-RXD-05's own CITATION of REQ-RXD-13 for a post-validation echo. V8 distinguishes: post-validated name echo -> wide marked elementNameEcho bound; unvalidated module-specifier echo -> STAYS at REQ-RXD-13's 16-char hostile cap. Neither class weakened. Scenarios minted: 05.16 (value-namespace battery, all 8 declaration kinds), 05.17 (negative boundary: local type/interface does NOT collide), 05.18 (claimedNameTail echo). REQ-RXD-09.6 amended in place (same ID). All 15 REQ-IDs survive; 05.1-.15 unchanged in outcome. PRIOR: V7 signed 2026-07-17 (claimed-name unification); V6 signed 2026-07-17 (F-1); V5 signed 2026-07-17 (council pass); V4 signed 2026-07-16.
verify_final_iter3: Iteration 3 at 9a1c23c: **pass-with-followups — ARCHIVE-READY, and this token is CORRECT, not a shortfall**. ORCHESTRATOR ERROR CORRECTED BY THE VERIFIER: the launch prompt asserted 'archive requires verify-report = pass'. That is FALSE — sdd-archive/SKILL.md:44 accepts BOTH `pass` and `pass-with-followups`, and the token is load-bearing in the OPPOSITE direction: Step 9 (SKILL.md:392-417) registers followups into project/pending-changes ONLY on `pass-with-followups`, and explicitly SKIPS that step on `pass`. Reporting `pass` with F-2..F-8 open would have SILENTLY DROPPED EIGHT tracked followups. The verifier refused the premise and cited the file. Do not 'upgrade' this verdict to `pass` — that would destroy the followup registration. F-1 RESOLVED: mechanical set diff reproduced from the spec's own V6 prose (spec 48 / code 48 / missing [] / extra [] / EXACT MATCH true, both directions); REQ-RXD-06.9 implemented with substring counter-check 5/0; reproduced at the PUBLIC API beyond the validator (addImport('eval'|'arguments') rejects, zero directives, file byte-unchanged; lookalikes accepted and emit). V6 COMPLETENESS CLAIM HOLDS, attacked not nodded: oracle = node's real ES module parser via vm.SourceTextModule; ~194.7M candidates, ZERO disagreements both directions (exhaustive len 1-4 over the full regex alphabet = 14.4M, exhaustive len 5 = 11.9M, len 6 partial 368/676 prefixes = 168M, plus 236k curated covering every ES/TS keyword, contextual keyword and global). No 49th exists. HONEST LIMIT stated by the verifier: len >=7 rests on the curated tier, not exhaustion — the sweeps corroborate the deduction, they do not replace it. TDD PASS: RED reproduced in a scratch worktree by reverting the two entries -> 3 pass / 2 fail, both on toBeInstanceOf(Error) receiving undefined — i.e. the validator not throwing, the defect itself. Not module resolution, not syntax. Step 11b GATING: clean, 0 Bug/Architecture/MAJOR. Suite 1829 pass / 0 fail, 181 files, 20.32s in a fresh /private/tmp worktree, ZERO environmental timeouts.
verify_final_f1_closure: F-1 CLOSED 2026-07-17 against owner-signed V6 — commits 9a92944 (fix) + 299fc32 (docs). IMPORT_RESERVED_WORDS 46 -> 48. Set verified MECHANICALLY, not asserted: the runtime Set was parsed and diffed against the spec's own 48-entry enumeration -> 'spec count: 48, code count: 48, missing: [], extra: [], EXACT MATCH: true'. TDD was a GENUINE RED->GREEN (the values were accepted before, so real RED was available and no probe substituted for it): scoped run pre-fix 3 pass / 2 fail, both failing for the right reason (toBeInstanceOf(Error) receiving undefined); post-fix 5 pass / 0 fail. Suite 1829 pass / 0 fail in a fresh /private/tmp worktree = 1824 baseline + 5 net new, exact, no unexplained delta, no environmental timeout observed. Scope discipline held: only IMPORT_RESERVED_WORDS + the REQ-RXD-06.9 test touched. Iteration 3 re-verify pending to convert pass-with-followups -> pass.

Suite 1824 pass / 0 fail in a fresh /private/tmp worktree; tsc 0 errors; build exit 0; NO environmental timeout observed. TDD Cycle Adherence: PASS — the near-miss-mutant amendment is HONOURED and the verifier reproduced BOTH legs himself (baseline 76/0; mutant + old assertion 76/0 = false pass; mutant + new assertion 65/11 = killed). The apply record RETRACTS its own false discrimination claim in place rather than quietly editing it — the correct disposal. The disclosed 5749bf2 commit-message deviation ruled RECOVERABLE: it misleads about which commit to read, never about what shipped. adversarial_review: REQUIRED (L + sensitive) — judgment-day BLIND has NOT yet run.
verify_final_open_decision: F-1 (owner's call, NOT gating): addImport('arguments') and addImport('eval') emit code that node rejects with `SyntaxError: Unexpected eval or arguments in strict mode`. Same class as SEC-1, narrowed to 2 identifiers. SPEC-CONFORMANT — REQ-RXD-06 says 'at minimum' and these are not reserved *words* — so the verifier reported rather than routed. But it sits in the channel the spec itself titles 'security — load-bearing', and his brute-force sweep proves that closing it makes the denylist PROVABLY COMPLETE at 48/48. Options: sdd-spec V6 -> sdd-apply for zero residual, or register as a pending-change.
carry_forward: REQ-RXD-11.5 wording amendment -> pending-changes at archive.

Strict TDD ordering violation on S-002 (impl written same editing pass as tests; RED proven retroactively) is DISCLOSED, not resolved — sdd-verify --mode=final MUST audit it in TDD Cycle Adherence. Do not let it downgrade to clean by attrition.

RESOLVED 2026-07-17 by diagnostic — full-suite instability is ENVIRONMENTAL, not a regression and not parallelism. The 'parallel-execution flakiness' hypothesis is REFUTED: bun test is sequential by default and package.json:64 runs bare `bun test` with no flags; nothing was ever parallel. Control experiment: HEAD's exact code (6ee9af0) checked out into /private/tmp gives 1779 pass / 0 fail, 3/3 runs (~23s) — so apply-progress.md's '1779/0' claim is truthful and exactly reproducible, just not under ~/Documents. Mechanism: subprocess-bound tests (bun install / bun link / tsc --noEmit via blocking spawnSync) run against bun's default 5000ms timeout while first-touch file scanning under ~/Documents inflates the first `tsc --noEmit` from 663ms to 16694ms (26x). tsc --extendedDiagnostics proves identical work both sides (256 files / 48335 symbols / 556 types). Strong inference: Microsoft Defender (wdavdaemon) real-time scan; NOT A/B-proven (corporate EDR cannot be disabled to confirm). Ruled out: parallelism, regression, ts-morph/type-graph growth, dist/testing/commons d.ts drift, core AST leakage (FIT-37 holds), iCloud sync, port binding, process.cwd() mutation. NO shared-state defect exists.

OPEN (pre-existing debt, NOT react-dialect scope): subprocess-bound tests declare no explicit timeout — test/docs/quickstart-docs.test.ts:312 and :319, plus the S-03 permissive-proof CI guard and the installed-consumer-vantage e2e leg. Their measured work is ~2s even on a fast filesystem against a 5000ms budget; the declared budget does not describe the work regardless of Defender. Fix = explicit per-test timeouts (~30 min). Do NOT raise the global timeout (masks genuinely slow tests). Environment-level companion fix (not committed): Defender real-time-scan exclusion for the repo or at minimum .tmp-*/ scratch dirs.
judgment_day: JUDGMENT: APPROVED 2026-07-17 at HEAD 0ec177c, Round 3, after 2 fix iterations. ROUND 1 (two blind judges): 3 real addImport defects (1 CRITICAL: type-only silent no-op; cross-module dup; aliased silent no-op) + 2 confirmed (16-char echo truncation; find('.tsx') vs JSDoc) -> spec V7 (claimed-name unification; orchestrator's Defect-3 premise corrected by sdd-spec: import { Foo as x, x } is NOT legal) + fix iteration 1. ROUND 2: CRITICAL confirmed by both judges — V7's claimed check scanned only import declarations, missing the VALUE NAMESPACE (8/8 declaration kinds emitted duplicate bindings; the correct predicate assertNoCollision existed 20 lines below TS's own addImport, ADR-0039-ratified, unused). Plus claimedNameTail reintroducing the 16-char echo bug its own file diagnoses. -> owner-ratified diagnosis: three rounds found the same class on new axes because fixes derived the model EMPIRICALLY while the complete model was already written; spec V8 ADOPTS ADR-0039 (CLAIMED = import half + value-namespace half) + fix iteration 2 (isValueNamespaceClaimed = assertNoCollision verbatim; 9-kill guard vs 6-7 floor). ROUND 3: BOTH JUDGES CLEAN of CRITICALs and real WARNINGs. Two unconfirmed SUGGESTIONs, both registered as followups, deliberately NOT fixed: (1) set-key-safety scan heuristics — regex matches only 4 hardcoded variable names, and the //-comment strip is greedy past string literals containing //; test-only defense-in-depth, runtime code verified correct via .has()/===/.includes() only; (2) self-alias import { X as X } + addImport(X) -> false-positive collision reject where no-op is ideal — SPEC-CONFORMANT (Step 1 requires UNALIASED; a self-alias is syntactically aliased), extremely rare, would need its own spec touch. Round-3 bonus verifications: destructuring/using/overload-set collisions correctly reject (beyond spec); nested scopes and function params correctly do NOT; reserved-word completeness re-swept against node's parser (zero gaps, zero over-restriction); suite 1860/0.
judgment_day_round_2_fix: Fix iteration 2 complete against owner-signed V8, commits b5f6e93/eb96646/ce4c8c3/6d89ceb/f4bc3f5. ADOPTED, not invented: isValueNamespaceClaimed = assertNoCollision's predicate verbatim (typescript/ops.ts:54-75), ORed with V7's import scan. All 8 declaration kinds reject; type/interface negative boundary creates. claimedNameTail -> elementNameEcho (post-validation name full and uncut; module-specifier echo stays at the 16-char hostile cap). Set-key-safety regex tolerates whitespace after '{' with self-test extended both directions. 4MiB conformance sample now 77,673 real <li> elements (parse 141ms->245ms, reported). THE BAR: value-namespace neuter kills 9 tests (floor was 6-7; V7's own guard once killed 1). Genuine RED on every behavioural fix (9 fail/44 pass -> 53/0; regex self-test 2 fail -> 84/0). Suite 1860/0 in a fresh /private/tmp worktree = 1844 + 16 net new. src/dialects/typescript/** at ZERO diff lines, verified. PENDING: Round 3 re-judge, both judges, blind, fresh, PARALLEL. Protocol: this was fix iteration 2 — if Round 3 still finds confirmed real issues, ASK THE OWNER before iterating further (never escalate automatically).
verify_final_iter4: Iteration 4 at 7eeb36c: pass-with-followups, ARCHIVE-READY (do NOT upgrade to pass — Step 9 suppression). adversarial_review: required AND ALREADY DISCHARGED (judgment-day 3 rounds -> APPROVED at 0ec177c; the blind gate does not re-run before archive). Code is EXACTLY V8: isValueNamespaceClaimed adopts ADR-0039's predicate byte-for-byte, ORed with V7's import scan — a strict superset of the TS dialect's own import-collision coverage. ADR-01 contract verified INTACT after two fix rounds: src/dialects/typescript/** diff EMPTY in main..HEAD. Step 11b: 0 gating. TDD PASS with both mutations reproduced by the verifier (value-namespace neuter kills 9/44, import-half neuter kills 6/47, boundaries 05.13/05.17 correctly survive both). Suite 1860/0 fresh /private/tmp worktree, zero environmental timeouts. LEDGER GAP FOUND AND CLOSED: four followups lived only in this orchestrator mirror (not a home sdd-archive reads) — the verifier consolidated the COMPLETE 18-ITEM LEDGER into verify-report.md §9, the canonical Step-9 payload: F-2..F-8, two round-3 suggestions, DOC-3, ARCH-2, subprocess-timeout debt, element-grammar gap, getAttribute first-match, SHARPENED TS-dialect addImport debt (can EMIT INVALID BINDINGS, distinct from injection item 22), TS-dialect trust-boundary JSDoc, default/mixed-import, .tsx/.jsx fold-or-reaffirm. F-1 and REQ-RXD-11.5 CLOSED. design.md:3 now FOUR versions stale (says V4, spec is V8) — F-4 widened, registered in §9.
